title: Ensure 'Basic Authentication Publishing Credentials' are  'Disabled'

input_row.title: Ensure 'Basic Authentication Publishing Credentials' are  'Disabled'
input_row.description: Basic Authentication Publishing Credentials provides the ability to publish—or deploy to—a function app deployment slot without a centralized Identity Provider. For a more effective, capable, and secure solution for Identity, Authentication, Authorization, and Accountability, a centralized Identity Provider such as Entra ID is strongly advised. This recommendation applies to function apps using the Consumption, Premium, or Dedicated (App Service) plans, which support deployment slots.
input_row.rationale: Basic Authentication introduces an identity silo for privileged access to a resource and produces logging which may not provide a full chain of accountability. This can be exploited in numerous ways and represents a significant vulnerability and attack vector.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, ensure that SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials and FTP Basic Auth Publishing Credentials are set to Off. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list function apps: az functionapp list For each function app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az functionapp deployment slot list --resource-group -- name For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the basic authentication for FTP setting: az resource show --resource-group --name ftp -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies -- parent sites//slots/ --query properties.allow Ensure that false is returned. For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the basic authentication for SCM setting: az resource show --resource-group --name scm -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies -- parent sites//slots/ --query properties.allow Ensure that false is returned.
input_row.remediation: CAUTION: Applying changes may cause your App Service resource to restart. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, set SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials and FTP Basic Auth Publishing Credentials to Off. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to disable basic authentication for FTP: az resource update --resource-group --name ftp -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies -- parent sites//slots/ --set properties.allow=false For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to disable basic authentication for SCM: az resource update --resource-group --name scm -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type basicPublishingCredentialsPolicies -- parent sites//slots/ --set properties.allow=false
input_row.impact: Disabling 'Basic Auth Publishing Credentials' will prevent the following deployment methods from working: FTP Basic Auth Publishing Credentials: • FTP • FTPS SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials: • Local Git • GitHub • Azure Repos • Bitbucket • Visual Studio (Version 17.12 and earlier) If this recommendation cannot be implemented because one of the above listed deployment methods is necessary and cannot be adapted, compensating controls (e.g. using FTPS Only and disabling only "SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials") are recommended to reduce potential attack surface. An Identity Provider that can be used by the function app deployment slot for authenticating users is required.
input_row.default_value: Basic authentication is enabled by default.
input_row.justification: The control in question pertains to Azure-specific settings related to function app deployment slots and basic authentication, which do not have a direct equivalent in AWS Lambda. AWS Lambda does not support deployment slots or basic authentication in the same manner as Azure App Services. Therefore, the audit cannot be automated using AWS APIs or CLI commands as described in the first opinion. The second opinion correctly identifies this mismatch and suggests that the control is not applicable to AWS in its current form.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Review the AWS Lambda function configurations manually via the AWS Management Console. 2. Navigate to the AWS Lambda service and select the function you wish to inspect. 3. Check the function's environment variables and IAM roles to ensure that no basic authentication mechanisms are implemented. 4. Confirm that the function's permissions and access controls align with AWS best practices for security and do not rely on basic authentication methods. 5. Document any findings and ensure that the function's security settings are consistent with AWS security guidelines.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Log in to the Azure Portal.**
   - Go to [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com/).

2. **Navigate to App Services or Function App.**
   - In the left-hand menu, select "App Services" or "Function App."

3. **Select the function app you wish to audit.**
   - Click on the name of the function app.

4. **Access Deployment Slots.**
   - Under the "Deployment" section in the left menu, click on "Deployment slots."

5. **Select each deployment slot for inspection.**
   - Click on the name of each deployment slot one by one.

6. **Check the Configuration settings.**
   - Under "Settings," click on "Configuration."

7. **Review General settings.**
   - In the General settings pane, look for "Platform settings."
   - Ensure that:
     - **SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials** is set to **Off**.
     - **FTP Basic Auth Publishing Credentials** is also set to **Off**.

8. **Repeat for each deployment slot.**
   - Continue steps 5-7 for each deployment slot that exists under the app service.

9. **Document findings and corrective actions.**
   - Record whether the configurations are compliant. If any of the credentials are still enabled, note the deployment slots that require remediation.

**Expected secure configuration:**
- Both SCM Basic Auth Publishing Credentials and FTP Basic Auth Publishing Credentials must be set to Off for all deployment slots.

**How to confirm compliance:**
- Ensure that the settings are not just visually compliant but also cross-verify through the Azure CLI commands provided if necessary, ensuring nothing is misconfigured. 

By following these steps, you can adequately audit the compliance of Basic Authentication Publishing Credentials for Azure Function App deployment slots.
